title: salvia lavandulifolia subsp. mesatlantica
intro: Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. mesatlantica, also known as mesatlantic salvia, is a subspecies of the Salvia lavandulifolia plant, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving cognitive function, and supporting respiratory health, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Salvia lavandulifolia subsp. mesatlantica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
